$comment: Sintrix sits on top of ha-mcp (https://github.com/homeassistant-ai/ha-mcp).
DEFAULT below = local stdio server running on the integrator's machine, connecting to the house
over the network. Works for BOTH remote and on-site houses — point HOMEASSISTANT_URL at the
right Home Assistant. Requires uv (https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) installed.
Credentials are loaded from .env via 'uv run --env-file .env' — fill in .env and you're done.
OS environment variables take priority over .env if both exist (standard dotenv behaviour).
If auth fails after editing .env, a stale OS env var may be overriding it — see README Troubleshooting.
The builder flags stay in env (not secrets); they are governed by project/02-guardrails.md.
ALTERNATIVE = HA add-on / remote HTTP: replace this server with
{ "type": "http", "url": "<MCP URL from add-on logs>" } and enable the feature flags
in the add-on's settings panel instead of here. Use the ha-mcp setup wizard for exact values:
